note: Initial roster from the 17 July 2026 deep research (38-agent sweep, every creator verified against live pages or Google-indexed profile snapshots that day; source detail per creator in sourceNote). The three-times-daily radar keeps it fresh from here.

creators.name: Gohar Khan
creators.niche: Study tips + college admissions (the biggest study-advice short-form creator in the world)
creators.whatTheyPost: 30 to 40 second single-tip videos for academically ambitious school students: do homework faster, memorise better, hack multiple-choice tests, write essays, find motivation for school. Also taxes/life-skills explainers. MIT grad credibility is baked into every video. Roughly 10M followers across platforms by age 25 (Forbes, Apr 2025).
creators.signatureFormats: Visual storytelling with colour, movement and simplicity: he opens on a relatable scenario or a physical prop (often food) as the hook, then delivers one tip in one take. His breakout 2020 to 21 era format was narrated tabletop videos with DIY paper cut-outs (grew him 30K to 100K in a week, per Fortune). Every second is scripted; no assumption the viewer knows anything. Shorts are his largest revenue driver (Forbes, Apr 2025).
creators.stealNotes: The closest model for Waleed: a credentialed insider (MIT vs NHS doctor + top A-level grades) giving one concrete tip per video with a physical prop hook. His revenue mix (50% sponsors, 25% AdSense, 25% his admissions consultancy Next Admit) shows short-form feeding a paid education service, exactly
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 3.8M: triple-sourced. (1) Wikipedia infobox states 3.80M as of 30 May 2026; (2) the Google-indexed TikTok profile snippet for tiktok.com/@goharsguide showed "3.8M followers, 135.2M likes" when searched on 17 July 2026; (3) Motion's creator ads page (motionapp.com, page dated 2026) lists "3.8M TikTok followers". Famous Birthdays also says "more than 3.8 million". One conflicting figure exists: his own Threads bio still says "TikTok: goharsguide (2.9M+)", which is a stale self-written bio, not evidence of a drop. Instagram 1.4M: Wikipedia (1.40M as of 30 May 2026) and Motion ("1.4M Meta f
creators.notes: Everything in the original brief checks out and the numbers are current. As of mid-2026 he is still the biggest study-advice short-form creator: 3.8M TikTok + 1.4M Instagram + 7.65M YouTube (5.38B views) + 191K Threads, roughly 12.85M total, matching the ~12.84M "total audience" figure on hafi.pro (June 2026). His flywheel has matured since the 2025 Forbes piece in ways directly relevant to Waleed: (1) the one-tip video now feeds THREE paid products, not one: Next Admit (admissions consulting), a weekly newsletter "The Studious Student" (thestudiousstudent.beehiiv.com), and a productivity app

creators.name: Olivia (Biology with Olivia)
creators.niche: GCSE and A-level Biology
creators.whatTheyPost: Exam question walkthroughs, memory tricks, common mistakes, mark-scheme pattern-spotting, revision strategy for biology. 339.4K followers, 9M likes per TikTok profile snippet observed 17 Jul 2026. Former classroom teacher who left after 3 years to teach online full time; sells £15 group masterclasses (with £5 free-school-meals rate) and notes via biologywitholivia.co.uk.
creators.signatureFormats: Talking-head 'do this to get full marks' mark-scheme breakdowns; personal confession posts ('I got a C on my final mocks before my A*'); comment-a-keyword lead capture ('comment FREE SAMPLE')
creators.cadence: Daily posting (her own claim: 'Every single day I post')
creators.stealNotes: The closest full business model to Waleed's: free daily exam-technique clips feeding paid group classes, with comment-keyword funnels. Her 'diagnose the mark scheme' framing is nearly identical to his 'diagnose before you treat' voice. She covers GCSE AND A-level; he can own the A-level-only lane.
creators.sourceNote: All counts observed 17 Jul 2026. TIKTOK (@biologywitholivia): confirmed live, verified through the profile URL plus multiple video pages. Follower count comes from Google/Bing search-result snippets of the TikTok profile, which returned two closely-spaced readings on the same day: "340.2K followers, 9M likes" and "339.4K / 9M likes." I recorded 340,200 as the freshest snippet; treat it as roughly 340K with a ~1K margin. I could NOT reach a third-party stat site to pin it exactly: Social Blade (socialblade.com/tiktok/user/biologywitholivia) returned 403, urlebird 403, exolyt 404/redirect, tokco
creators.notes: CLOSEST FULL BUSINESS MODEL to Waleed's, hence relevance 5. Model to study, not a head-on competitor (she is biology-only, GCSE + A-level; he is A-level across subjects, so the A-level-only lane is still open to own). Verified funnel: free daily exam-technique clips feeding paid £15 90-minute Zoom masterclasses (confirmed on video 7361035192238099744: '£15 - includes the zoom webinar, notes, checklist & recording', with a £5 free-school-meals rate on emailed evidence), plus model-answer notes/courses sold via biologywitholivia.co.uk and stan.store/biologywitholivia. Authority stack mirrors Wal

creators.name: Dr Justin Sung
creators.niche: Learning science / evidence-based study skills
creators.whatTheyPost: Former medical doctor turned learning coach (co-founder of iCanStudy). Evidence-based learning strategy: encoding before review, schema-building, mind-mapping (his GRINDE method), myth-busting popular techniques like flashcards and rereading. Long-form YouTube first, with Shorts cutdowns.
creators.signatureFormats: Whiteboard/talking-head explainer with a contrarian, myth-busting hook ("stop doing X, it's low-efficiency"), then the cognitive-science reason, then the replacement technique. Ranks techniques by efficiency the way Waleed ranks by yield. His TEDx talk "Stop Studying. Start Learning" is the thesis statement of the whole channel.
creators.stealNotes: The strongest philosophical match to A-Level Accelerators' "top grades are a system, not a talent". Steal the myth-bust structure: name a technique students trust (highlighting, rereading, even flashcards), explain with doctor's authority why it is low-yield, prescribe the high-yield replacement (bl
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 269.6K: from Google-indexed snapshots of tiktok.com/@drjustinsung ("Cognitive performance coach Ex-Doctor", 4.4M likes), observed 17 Jul 2026 across three separate search results; one slightly older snapshot read 268.9K, so the figure is consistent to within a rounding error. Instagram 107K: from Google-indexed snapshots of instagram.com/@drjustinsung (bio: "Co-Founder of @icanstudy, Ex-Doctor, 2.1M on YouTube", 165 following, 885 posts), observed 17 Jul 2026 in two independent search results. Direct fetches of TikTok and Instagram are JS-blocked and Socialblade/HypeAuditor returned 403
creators.notes: Both handles verified against his official Linktree. The strategic finding for Waleed: Sung's 2.17M-subscriber YouTube authority has NOT translated proportionally to short-form (269.6K TikTok, 107K Instagram, roughly 8x and 20x smaller), because his TikTok is mostly Shorts cutdowns ending in "check my bio" CTAs that earn only hundreds to ~3K likes; the account reads as a funnel appendage, not a native TikTok play. His only true TikTok breakout was personal doctor-identity content, not study technique. Lesson: lead short-form with the doctor story and the contrarian myth-bust hook, and make vid

creators.name: Kate (Study With Kate)
creators.niche: GCSE-to-A-level student creator: 11 grade 9s at GCSE, now doing A-levels aiming for medicine at Cambridge; inventor of the viral 'revision roulette' spreadsheet method
creators.whatTheyPost: Revision roulette walkthroughs (a Google Sheets randomiser covering every GCSE subject, so widely copied that Tes hosts teacher templates of it), study organisation, GCSE science '9s' technique videos, study vlogs and motivation. 6.6M TikTok likes. Sells revision materials via Buy Me a Coffee. No verified Instagram found; follower count therefore null.
creators.signatureFormats: Spreadsheet-on-screen method explainers; study-with-me; tips talking to camera
creators.stealNotes: Closest stylistic match to Waleed's 'top grades are a system' positioning, from the student side. A named method (revision roulette) became her brand; Waleed's Blurt and Fix / diagnostic system deserves the same named-method treatment in reels.
creators.sourceNote: TikTok: 182,400 followers (with 10.2M likes, 60 following) observed FIRST-HAND on the live profile https://www.tiktok.com/@study_with_kate on 17 Jul 2026 via browser. Corroborated by Famous Birthdays which says "more than 180,000 followers" (accessed 17 Jul 2026; the site does not date its figure). The 157.1K in the input dossier is stale; she has added roughly 25K followers since that observation. Instagram: null because no verified account exists. Her TikTok bio links only an email and Buy Me a Coffee (no Instagram), her Buy Me a Coffee page lists no socials, and the Instagram handles surfac
creators.notes: Account fully verified first-hand. Live profile (17 Jul 2026): @study_with_kate, 182.4K followers, 10.2M likes, bio "GCSEs, A levels, study tips and motivation" with contact email and Buy Me a Coffee shop link. Playlists: "Study roulette" (36 posts) and "Day in the life" (28 posts). Timeline: started TikTok March 2024 (Famous Birthdays); the study roulette spreadsheet video around July 2024 made the account; achieved eleven grade 9s at GCSE (results reaction with her mum, Aug 2025; thank-you follow-up 25 Aug 2025 got 12.4K likes); now doing A-levels including biology and chemistry, publicly ai

creators.name: Miss Estruch (Katharine Estruch)
creators.niche: A-level Biology (the most A-level-focused big creator found)
creators.whatTheyPost: A-level biology exam technique: 'do this to get full marks' mark-point breakdowns, common exam mistakes, AQA essay planning, required-practical prep, examiner tips. Biology teacher with 15+ years experience, MA in Education, also a YouTuber and author; sells A-level notes with examiner tips via missestruch.co.uk. 100.2K followers per TikTok snippet observed 17 Jul 2026.
creators.signatureFormats: Mark-scheme anatomy videos ('here are the key marking points for this gas exchange question'); comment-keyword funnels ('comment FREE SAMPLE', 'comment ESSAY for my free webinar'); long-form on YouTube, technique clips on TikTok
creators.stealNotes: The best direct benchmark for Waleed: A-level-only, technique-led, funnels TikTok comments into free webinars then paid resources. At 100K she proves an A-level-specific (not GCSE) audience of this size exists on TikTok. Waleed can differentiate on multi-subject systems + doctor credibility vs her s
creators.sourceNote: TikTok @miss.estruch.biology: 100.2K followers and 3.2M likes, from Google search-result snippets of the live TikTok profile, observed 17 Jul 2026 (consistent across three separate searches). The TikTok video and profile pages do not render for direct fetch (server returns only the generic "TikTok - Make Your Day" shell), so these come from search snippets, not a directly loaded page; I could not source them from a third-party stat site (Social Blade profile 403'd). Instagram @missestruchbiology: real name "Katharine Estruch | A-level & GCSE Biology Teacher", ~20K followers and 1,989 posts, fr
creators.notes: Real name Katharine Estruch; UK secondary Biology teacher of 15 to 16+ years, MA in Education, contracted author for Cambridge University Press (AS/A-level Biology workbook and flashcards). Single-subject: A-level (and some GCSE) Biology, AQA and OCR. Sells via missestruch.co.uk: A-level notes with examiner tips, The Biology Study Club membership, online courses, Pick 'N' Mix live tutoring from £18, and 2026 practice papers. Runs regular TikTok/Instagram LIVEs (weekdays 12:30pm, Thursdays 8pm) and an Easter/exam-season "Bootcamp". Funnel is clear and repeatable: technique/prediction clip on TikTo

creators.name: Hazel Lindsey (Science with Hazel)
creators.niche: Tutor-creator: Cambridge graduate and qualified teacher covering GCSE, IGCSE and A-level Biology, Chemistry and Physics; MD of SwH Learning
creators.whatTheyPost: Fast-paced science recaps, top exam tips, last-minute cramming content, promotion of her revision courses and 'The Perfect Answer' guides. Recommended in MidKent College's 2025 revision-accounts list.
creators.signatureFormats: Quick-fire exam-tip reels, topic recaps, exam-season countdown content
creators.stealNotes: The closest business-model twin to A-Level Accelerators: credentialed expert using social content to sell her own courses and guides rather than chasing creator revenue. Her Instagram is small, which suggests the tutor-to-Instagram playbook is still unclaimed at scale.
creators.sourceNote: All figures observed 17 Jul 2026. TikTok 25,700 followers (also 449,400 total likes, 501 videos) read live from the TikTok profile page's embedded __UNIVERSAL_DATA_FOR_REHYDRATION__ JSON via direct fetch. TikTok nickname "Science with Hazel", bio "Science Youtuber 82k subs, Cambridge grad, qualified science teacher", bioLink www.swhlearning.co.uk. The brief's 25.4K was slightly stale; live is 25.7K. Instagram 20,925 followers (1,162 posts, following 1,155) read live from Instagram's web_profile_info API (app-id 936619743392459); full name "Hazel Lindsey", verified as a business/professional ac
creators.notes: CLOSEST BUSINESS-MODEL TWIN to A-Level Accelerators and the reason relevance is 5: a credentialed UK expert (Cambridge grad, qualified/PGCE science teacher, MD of SwH Learning) using short-form social to sell her OWN courses, private tuition and 'The Perfect Answer' revision guides, not to chase creator ad revenue. Same playbook Waleed is starting: teacher-founder -> free syllabus recaps -> owned products. Differences: she is science (Bio/Chem/Physics) across GCSE/IGCSE/A-level/IB, Waleed is broad A-level; she leans YouTube-first (her biggest asset), with TikTok/IG as secondary. KEY STEAL/CAUT

creators.name: Neil Trivedi (NeilDoesMaths)
creators.niche: GCSE and A-level maths; MyEdSpace lead teacher
creators.whatTheyPost: Maths walkthroughs and exam content as the face of MyEdSpace, the direct competitor with the biggest teacher-brand machine.
creators.signatureFormats: Teacher-named account fronting a company funnel
creators.stealNotes: The named-face-over-brand-account proof: MyEdSpace corporate account sits near 6.4K while the teacher accounts carry the reach. Watch how they route short-form into paid classes.
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 216.7K observed 17 Jul 2026 in the niche research (playbook evidence); full verification pending first sweep.
creators.notes: Added from the research watchlist; sweep should verify handle and numbers first-hand.

creators.name: Dr Karan Rajan (Karan Rangarajan)
creators.niche: Medical myth-busting and health education (NHS surgeon)
creators.whatTheyPost: 60-second myth-busts and 'weird medical facts': sleep, gut health, viral wellness trends, NHS life. TikTok 5.4M followers and about 364M likes (observed on his TikTok profile via search, July 2026); Instagram 2M (observed on his IG profile via search, July 2026); his own site claims 12M+ across all platforms. Started TikTok Nov 2019, first viral 'weird medical facts' video Feb 2020, filmed before a shift and blew up while he was in a 3-hour surgery.
creators.signatureFormats: (1) Green-screen explainer: him in scrubs in front of a source article or diagram. (2) Stitch/duet debunk: reacts to a viral health claim, then corrects it with the science and dry British humour. (3) Series content ('weird medical facts', 'school with Dr Karan'). Credential is worn, not spoken: NHS scrubs on screen, 'Dr' in the handle, MRCS MBBS BSc listed in the Instagram display name. His stated hook philosophy (Async interview): when the content is genuinely useful 'it doesn't need a hook or a gimmick', lead with the surprising fact itself.
creators.stealNotes: The direct model for Waleed: NHS clothing as the visual credential, qualifications in the display name, and a repeatable named series. Swap health myths for revision myths: stitch bad study-hack videos and correct them with 'diagnose before you treat' framing.
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 5,400,000: observed via Google search snippets of the live TikTok profile (tiktok.com/@dr.karanr) on 17 July 2026, which read "5.4M Followers, 364.1M Likes" (one fresher snippet showed 364.7M likes); independently corroborated by third-party estimator hafi.pro, whose June 2026 data lists exactly 5,400,000. Instagram 2,000,000: the rounded figure shown in the Google snippet of the live Instagram profile (instagram.com/drkaranrajan) observed 17 July 2026; hafi.pro (June 2026) lists a more precise 2,163,467. Direct WebFetch of both profiles is blocked (JS-rendered), and Socialblade returne
creators.notes: Both handles verified as the same person: the TikTok bio and TikTok's own newsroom creator spotlight name him as NHS general surgeon and University of Sunderland honorary lecturer, his May 2025 TikTok caption cross-promotes '@drkaranrajan' (his IG handle), and his own site drkaranrajan.com links both. Still highly active through 2026 (videos observed from Dec 2025 and media pickups from June 2026). Business evolution worth noting for Waleed: #1 Sunday Times bestseller ('This Book May Save Your Life'), the 'Dr Karan Explores' podcast, founder of @loamscience and Chief of Science at Throne Scien

creators.name: FreeGCSEMathsTeacher (anonymous UK maths teacher)
creators.niche: GCSE maths revision
creators.whatTheyPost: Free bite-size GCSE maths lessons: past-paper walkthroughs, hardest-question breakdowns, calculator hacks, statistics topics like estimate of the mean, forming and solving equations. Biggest single GCSE/A-level education account found on TikTok (2.8M followers, 29M likes per TikTok profile snippet observed 17 Jul 2026). The teacher stays anonymous; identity could not be sourced.
creators.signatureFormats: One question, one whiteboard/screen, one clean walkthrough in under 60 seconds; recurring 'hardest GCSE question' and 'calculator trick' hooks
creators.cadence: Very frequent, multiple short videos per week
creators.stealNotes: Proof that pure utility with zero personal brand can hit millions: the account is anonymous. For Waleed the lesson is the format, not the anonymity: single exam question, timed walkthrough, no fluff. Caution: the other mega maths account, Think Maths GCSE (625.7K), is a Think Academy brand account (
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 2.8M followers / 29M likes / 2 following: from the Google-indexed TikTok profile snippet for tiktok.com/@freegcsemathsteacher, observed 17 Jul 2026 via two independent search queries in this session (matches the prior researcher's 17 Jul 2026 observation). Cross-check: an Irish Star (Reach plc) article dated December 2024 cited 2.5 million followers, consistent with growth to 2.8M by mid-2026. Every third-party stat site was inaccessible for direct confirmation (Socialblade 403, Exolyt 404, Countik loaded zeroes, Urlebird 403), so the 2.8M figure ultimately traces to TikTok's own profil
creators.notes: Account confirmed real and genuinely anonymous (no name found anywhere, including a Dec 2024 Irish Star press mention). Multi-platform: YouTube channel youtube.com/@freegcsemathsteacher ('FREE GCSE Maths Teacher', offers free live GCSE revision) and a Facebook page also exist; no subscriber numbers retrievable for either. Recency caveat: the newest Google-indexed video dates to ~11 Jun 2025 (ID timestamp), so current mid-2026 posting cadence could not be confirmed from indexed data; this is likely index lag, but worth a manual profile check before citing cadence. Relevance 4/5 for Waleed: GCSE

creators.name: Matt Green (The Rapping Science Teacher)
creators.niche: GCSE science (biology, chemistry, physics) via rap
creators.whatTheyPost: GCSE science raps over UK rap/drill soundtracks (Central Cee, Stormzy, Skepta, Dave beats) covering respiration, electrolysis and other spec topics. Owner/Director of JGM Science Tutors. 1.5M followers and 25.5M likes per TikTok profile snippet observed 17 Jul 2026 (an earlier Rutgers teacher-TikTok roundup had him at 790K, and a My South London feature at 200K, showing the growth curve). Won the TikTok Educator Award at the #1Billion Summit (reported Feb 2026) and signed a GCSE 'Rapping Up' revision guide series with DK Learning.
creators.signatureFormats: Full-production rap videos mapped to exam spec topics; term-time weekly release schedule that students anticipate like a series
creators.cadence: Weekly raps during term time (historically 'new raps every Friday at 4pm until Christmas')
creators.stealNotes: The strongest personal-brand example in UK EdTok: one unmistakable gimmick, industrial consistency, then monetised off-platform (books, tutoring). Waleed shouldn't rap, but the 'one recognisable format, same slot every week' mechanic transfers directly.
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 1,500,000: from the TikTok profile snippet indexed in search results ("1.5M Followers, 25.1M Likes" on tiktok.com/@mattgreen.jgm), observed 17 Jul 2026 across three separate searches; corroborated by Khaleej Times (event 10 to 11 Jan 2026) which reported 1.4M followers when he won the Educator Award, consistent with growth to 1.5M by July. Handle ownership confirmed directly via TikTok's oEmbed API, which returned author_name "Matt Green" and author_url tiktok.com/@mattgreen.jgm for his video 7513520992401116438. Instagram 687,000: from the Instagram profile snippet indexed in search resul
creators.notes: All core facts in the brief check out, with small updates. TikTok profile snippet now reads 25.1M likes (brief said 25.5M; both are Google-index snapshots of the same profile, minor variance between crawls). Award confirmed with full detail: Educator Award at the 1 Billion Followers Summit (4th edition), Dubai, 10 to 11 Jan 2026, $100,000 prize. DK Learning deal confirmed (The Bookseller, 9 Jun 2025, headline visible outside paywall); the three Rapping Up books (Biology, Chemistry, Physics) published 22 Jan 2026 and are on Amazon UK. Business behind the content, per his own site jgmtutors.com/mat

creators.name: Ali Abdaal
creators.niche: Evidence-based productivity and study techniques (doctor-creator archetype)
creators.whatTheyPost: Cambridge-trained doctor turned the world's biggest productivity creator. Study foundations: active recall, spaced repetition, Feynman technique, note-taking systems; now broader productivity and "feel-good productivity". Short-form repurposes the same evidence-based tips.
creators.signatureFormats: Calm, specific, research-backed explainers: "here's a thing that works, here's the evidence". No shouting, no drama. His growth model is consistency over virality (400+ videos compounding), which is a realistic model for a busy NHS doctor. Study-tips TikToks are direct-to-camera with text overlays citing the evidence.
creators.stealNotes: The proof that a UK doctor talking about evidence-based revision can become the category leader. Waleed's medical framing (high-yield/low-yield, diagnose before you treat) is the same move. Steal: cite the evidence on screen, keep delivery calm and credible, publish on a sustainable schedule rather
creators.sourceNote: TikTok 392.6K followers (plus 3.4M total likes): from Google-indexed TikTok profile data for tiktok.com/@aliabdaal, observed 17 July 2026; the handle's existence was independently confirmed via a tiktok.com domain-restricted search and TikTok's oEmbed API (author_unique_id "aliabdaal"). Instagram 1,000,000: Google-indexed Instagram profile data ("1M followers, 2,077 posts"), observed 17 July 2026; the South China Morning Post (published 28 Nov 2025) reported 1.1 million Instagram followers, so the true current figure is likely 1.0 to 1.1M+; the 1M milestone itself was announced ~22 April 2025
creators.notes: Both handles verified as belonging to the real Ali Abdaal (Cambridge-trained doctor turned productivity creator). Relevance 4 not 5: he is the archetype proof that a UK doctor doing evidence-based study content becomes category leader, and his 2018 to 2023 study-content era (active recall, spaced repetition, evidence-on-screen, credential-as-hook) is the exact playbook for Waleed. But his 2025 to 2026 short-form output has pivoted to business/entrepreneurship (Lifestyle Business Academy, pricing advice, calendar habits), so he is no longer a direct competitor in the A-level revision niche and his cu

creators.whatTheyPost: Reels and carousels on study routines, revision technique (blurting is a technique she popularised), burnout prevention, consistency, plus life/travel content since graduating. Recommended by MidKent College's 2025 revision-accounts list for 'Study Motivation & Mindset'.

creators.name: Ruby Granger

creators.whatTheyPost: Day-in-the-life study reels with soothing voiceover, exquisitely tidy notes, reading and library content, 12-hour study day vlogs cross-promoted from YouTube, book recommendations. Runs Pumpkin Productivity as a spin-off brand.

creators.stealNotes: Proof that pure study-atmosphere content builds huge loyal audiences. Waleed should not copy the aesthetic angle, but her day-structure format maps directly onto his revision-timetable and study-systems content.

creators.name: Ishaan Bhimjiyani (Revishaan)

creators.name: Hannah Kettle

creators.name: Sophie (my honest studyblr)
creators.niche: Studygram: handwritten revision notes, honest study advice, studygram how-tos; started as an A-level student account
creators.whatTheyPost: Neat handwritten notes photos, revision-technique carousels, 'how to start a studygram' guides, honest takes on study culture. Started August 2020 as an A-level student; now studies International Relations and Social Anthropology at St Andrews, so A-level output has slowed (profile showed only 22 visible posts when observed).
creators.signatureFormats: Note-photo carousels; text-heavy advice carousels; Q&A prompts in captions to drive comments
creators.stealNotes: Shows carousel-first (not reel-first) growth is possible in this niche. Follower count appears to have declined from ~193K to 167K as she left the A-level lane, evidence that the A-level audience churns every two years and rewards whoever stays.
creators.sourceNote: Instagram 167K: from the Google-indexed instagram.com/myhoneststudyblr profile snippet ("167K followers, 178 following, 22 posts"), observed 17 Jul 2026 and consistent across three separate search queries. TikTok: the handle @myhoneststudyblr exists (listed on her Linktree, fetched 17 Jul 2026, and a live video URL at tiktok.com/@myhoneststudyblr/video/7093463381121813766), but NO follower count could be sourced: TikTok's own page returned no profile data to an unauthenticated fetch, and Social Blade, HypeAuditor, Exolyt, Countik, Urlebird and Tikleap were all 403/404/placeholder, so null rath
creators.notes: Account confirmed real and current: display name "sophie studies", 22 years old, UK, now studying International Relations and Social Anthropology at St Andrews. Only 22 visible posts (most archived or deleted), which matches the earlier observation that her A-level-era output has slowed. Monetises via affiliate codes: her Linktree (fetched 17 Jul 2026) lists Amazon UK/US storefronts and discount codes for Tombow, Stationery Pal, Perlego, xTiles and roughly a dozen other study brands, plus YouTube, Pinterest, Threads and Tumblr all under the same handle. creators.stealNotes: Her printables-to-blog funnel is exactly Waleed's lead-magnet model (diagnostic, tracker, blurting template) executed on Instagram. Follower figure is single-source; verify on the profile before quoting it anywhere.
creators.sourceNote: Instagram 161K: from Google-indexed metadata of instagram.com/emily_studies itself, observed 17 Jul 2026 consistently across three separate search queries; still effectively SINGLE-ORIGIN because no independent tracker covers her (Social Blade returned 403/untracked for emily_studies, HypeAuditor has no report; socialveins.com tracks the different @emilystudying account at 36K). creators.notes: Account confirmed real and belongs to the right person (direct fetch returned profile page "Emily ✨ (@emily_studies)"; her blog emilystudies.co.uk links the handle). Her Instagram bio as indexed in Jul 2026 now reads "22, England, starting primary PGCE in September": she has pivoted from medical studies to primary teacher training, so GCSE/A-level exam content is receding, matching the brief's expectation. Her blog is essentially dormant (last substantive post 13 Oct 2020; a stray "test" post 26 Jun 2025 suggests minimal maintenance), though the studygram guide remains indexed. No recent posts

creators.name: Eve Bennett (Revision With Eve)
creators.niche: Legacy GCSE/A-level revision creator: built Revision With Eve (120K+ YouTube subs) alongside a lifestyle main channel, then pivoted to journalism
creators.whatTheyPost: Now mostly life and career content (Oxford grad, Sky News planning producer). Historically: subject-by-subject GCSE/A-level revision videos, exam-season study-with-me content, collaborations with BBC, Childline and Quizlet.
creators.signatureFormats: Talking-head revision explainers (YouTube era); now lifestyle reels
creators.stealNotes: Included as a market-gap signal, not a model to copy: the dedicated 'revision channel by a top student' slot she vacated has no dominant Instagram successor. Her old Revision With Eve format (one exam board, one paper, one video) is directly reusable as Reels.

creators.name: Andrew Bruff (Mr Bruff)
creators.niche: GCSE and A-level English (YouTube-first; small on TikTok)
creators.whatTheyPost: AQA GCSE English Language and Literature: set-text analysis (Macbeth, Jekyll and Hyde, A Christmas Carol), question-by-question exam guides. 453,000 YouTube subscribers but only 28.1K TikTok followers (both per search snippets observed 17 Jul 2026). Sells eBooks, revision guides and predictions-free practice papers via mrbruff.com. Recommended in both the MidKent College and Tutography 2025/2026 revision-account roundups.
creators.signatureFormats: Long-form structured YouTube lessons; TikTok used only as a clip channel
